prestazioni: Engine swap to a 4G93T (1.8L turbocharged inline-4) with forged internals: Mahle Motorsport forged pistons (87.5mm bore, 8.5:1 compression), Eagle H-beam connecting rods, and ACL Race Series bearings. Pair with a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger (0.63 A/R turbine housing) and a Tomei 260° camshaft kit for 18psi of boost, targeting 320hp at the crank.

Full custom 3-inch turbo-back exhaust system by HKS Hi-Power Spec-L, featuring a 304 stainless steel downpipe with a 200-cell GReddy Revolution RS catalytic converter, paired with a custom 3.5-inch straight-pipe section terminating in a Fujitsubo GEN-II Legalis R muffler for aggressive but street-legal sound.

Suspension overhaul with Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (Mitsubishi Carisma GT-specific valving), polyurethane bushings from SuperPro (part numbers SPF0115K, SPF0116K, SPF0117K), and Whiteline adjustable rear sway bar (24mm, part number BSR75XZ). Align to -2.5° front camber, -1.8° rear camber, and 5mm front toe-out for optimal turn-in response.
estetica: Custom widebody conversion using a Carisma GT-specific Rocket Bunny V1 kit (Pandem Design), extended 30mm per side with carbon fiber fenders, front bumper canards, and a duckbill rear spoiler. Paint in Mitsubishi’s original ‘Pyrenees Black’ (code 58K) with matte black accents on the hood and roof.

OEM+ interior refresh with Recaro SRD-E bucket seats (Mitsubishi Carisma GT-specific mounts), Takata Drift III 4-point harnesses, and a Nardi Classic 350mm steering wheel (part number 12013500) wrapped in Alcantara. Replace the stock gauge cluster with a Defi Advance BF series 60mm boost gauge, oil pressure gauge, and water temperature gauge in a custom A-pillar pod.

Lightweight 17x8.5 ET35 Enkei RS05RR wheels (Mitsubishi Carisma GT PCD 4x114.3) wrapped in 225/40R17 Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires. Brake upgrade to DBA 4000 Series rotors (vented, slotted, 280mm front, 260mm rear) with Ferodo DS2500 pads and Goodridge G-Stop braided stainless steel brake lines.
